title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots consider signing Tyreek Hill, despite his legal troubles and injury recovery, for his deep threat abilities.
Marquise Brown, known for his speed, is an option if Tyreek Hill's risks outweigh benefits for the Patriots.
Linebacker Bobby Okereke, noted for his defensive abilities, could strengthen Patriots' front seven if signed.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta led Boston's victory over the Wizards with 24 points, 10 rebounds, and two blocks.
Jayson Tatum played 32 minutes, recording 20 points and 14 rebounds but struggled to shoot from long range.
The Celtics faced shooting difficulties, with key players like Jaylen Brown and Payton Pritchard missing multiple 3-point attempts.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys began their St. Patrick's Day weekend with a concert at MGM Music Hall on March 13.
The show featured opening performances by The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown.
Highlights included Ken Casey engaging the crowd and Jeff DaRosa playing banjo and guitar.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC debuted in the NWSL with a 1-0 loss to Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium.
The match saw eight yellow cards given, tying an NWSL record, with Legacy's St-Georges getting a red card.
Over 30,000 fans attended, highlighting the enduring Boston vs. New York sports rivalry in Legacy's inaugural match.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off St. Patrick's Day weekend with a concert at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13.
The band performed a mix of old favorites and new songs, including protest track 'Citizen I.C.E.' against ICE policies.
Heather Fahey joined for a duet, while the band closed their encore with 'I’m Shipping Up to Boston' and 'The Big Man.'

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at 11, Bennett supports the Joslin Diabetes Center for providing essential tools and guidance.
Bennett aims to support diabetes research and inspire others by completing the 26.2-mile Boston Marathon route.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, a Springfield native, has been nominated for her fifth Oscar for her work on 'Sinners'.
She is the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history, and could win her third Oscar on Sunday.
Carter discusses her costume design process, including sourcing from Massachusetts, ahead of the 98th Oscars.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if voters reject a proposed property-tax override for funding.
The district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staffing and avoid service cuts.
Brookline voters will decide on the property-tax override at the May 5 annual town election.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in pet shops to reduce reliance on puppy mills.
Provisions include expanding access to pet ownership in state-aided housing and preventing insurance discrimination based on dog breeds.
The legislation is under consideration in Massachusetts, requiring approval from both legislative chambers before governor's approval.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: State officials report persistent drought in Massachusetts despite February's significant snowfall.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts elevated to Level 3 — Critical Drought, while Connecticut River Valley is at Level 2.
Residents are advised to conserve water and check for leaks to combat ongoing water shortages.
